findings.message: No output-format declared. Channel install skills that produce observable state changes (imports wired, pnpm install run) should document expected output so Claude can confirm success.
File has no YAML frontmatter at all — opens directly with '# Add Parallel AI Integration'. Missing required 'name' and 'description' fields; skill cannot be installed or referenced by name.

Uses sed -i.bak "s/^PARALLEL_API_KEY=.*/PARALLEL_API_KEY=${API_KEY_FROM_USER}/" — user-controlled variable interpolated directly into sed substitution. sed metacharacters (/, &, \) in the API key value can cause unexpected behavior or injection.
No output-format declared. Same as add-discord pattern — systematic across all channel install skills.
No output-format declared. Systematic gap across all add-<channel> skills.
No output-format declared.

Phase 3 instructs Claude to run 'sudo pfctl' and 'sudo tee' to modify system packet filtering rules. Errors in pfctl rules can break network connectivity system-wide; Claude lacks interactive confirmation context.
References v1 source paths that don't exist in v2: src/ipc.ts, src/channels/whatsapp.ts, src/db.ts, src/whatsapp-auth.ts, groups/CLAUDE.md. Will cause Claude to attempt edits on nonexistent files.

Instructs Claude to edit src/channels/whatsapp.ts directly. In v2, channel adapters are installed from the channels branch via /add-<channel> skills — not present on trunk by default. Creates false expectation.
Contains 6+ vague quantifiers: 'appropriate', 'relevant', 'some', 'various', 'sufficient', 'reasonable'. Each -2 under R29.

Instructs Claude to execute 'curl -fsSL onecli.sh/install | sh' and 'curl -fsSL onecli.sh/cli/install | sh' — remote code execution without checksum verification. If onecli.sh is compromised or hijacked, all operators running /init-onecli execute attacker code.
Contains 3 vague quantifiers: 'various', 'appropriate', and 'some'. Each -2 under R29.
Declares 'Compatibility: NanoClaw v1.0.0. Directory structure may change in future versions.' in a v2 repository. Creates ambiguity about whether the skill applies to the current version.
Stores full Chrome browser session to disk after X.com login. The session file grants full account access and must be treated as a credential. No security warning is provided to the operator.

Contains v1-era content (registered_groups.json, WhatsApp JID management, /workspace/ipc/ paths, ~/.config/nanoclaw/sender-allowlist.json, register_group MCP tool) mixed with v2 concepts. Main agent will attempt v1 operations against a v2 system.
References /workspace/ipc/available_groups.json and /workspace/ipc/tasks/ — paths from v1 file-based IPC system removed in v2. Main agent will not find these files and may emit errors.

'prepare': 'husky' runs on pnpm install. While husky is well-known and pnpm minimumReleaseAge/onlyBuiltDependencies policies mitigate the primary risk, the prepare hook is a known supply-chain attack vector.

execFileSync runs TypeScript files whose paths derive from process.argv[4] (newCorePath). A malicious newCorePath could execute arbitrary code. Risk is low-medium in practice as this is a developer/CI script, not agent-facing.
File has no YAML frontmatter at all — opens directly with '# Add Parallel AI Integration'. Missing required 'name' and 'description' fields; skill cannot be installed or referenced by name.
References v1 source paths that don't exist in v2: src/ipc.ts, src/channels/whatsapp.ts, src/db.ts, src/whatsapp-auth.ts, groups/CLAUDE.md. Will cause Claude to attempt edits on nonexistent files.

Contains v1-era content (registered_groups.json, WhatsApp JID management, /workspace/ipc/ paths, ~/.config/nanoclaw/sender-allowlist.json, register_group MCP tool) mixed with v2 concepts. Main agent will attempt v1 operations against a v2 system.

Instructs Claude to execute 'curl -fsSL onecli.sh/install | sh' and 'curl -fsSL onecli.sh/cli/install | sh' — remote code execution without checksum verification. If onecli.sh is compromised or hijacked, all operators running /init-onecli execute attacker code.

Phase 3 instructs Claude to run 'sudo pfctl' and 'sudo tee' to modify system packet filtering rules. Errors in pfctl rules can break network connectivity system-wide; Claude lacks interactive confirmation context.

Uses sed -i.bak "s/^PARALLEL_API_KEY=.*/PARALLEL_API_KEY=${API_KEY_FROM_USER}/" — user-controlled variable interpolated directly into sed substitution. sed metacharacters (/, &, \) in the API key value can cause unexpected behavior or injection.

'prepare': 'husky' runs on pnpm install. While husky is well-known and pnpm minimumReleaseAge/onlyBuiltDependencies policies mitigate the primary risk, the prepare hook is a known supply-chain attack vector.
Stores full Chrome browser session to disk after X.com login. The session file grants full account access and must be treated as a credential. No security warning is provided to the operator.

execFileSync runs TypeScript files whose paths derive from process.argv[4] (newCorePath). A malicious newCorePath could execute arbitrary code. Risk is low-medium in practice as this is a developer/CI script, not agent-facing.
No output-format declared. Channel install skills that produce observable state changes (imports wired, pnpm install run) should document expected output so Claude can confirm success.
No output-format declared. Same as add-discord pattern — systematic across all channel install skills.
No output-format declared. Systematic gap across all add-<channel> skills.
No output-format declared.

Declares 'Compatibility: NanoClaw v1.0.0. Directory structure may change in future versions.' in a v2 repository. Creates ambiguity about whether the skill applies to the current version.
References /workspace/ipc/available_groups.json and /workspace/ipc/tasks/ — paths from v1 file-based IPC system removed in v2. Main agent will not find these files and may emit errors.

Instructs Claude to edit src/channels/whatsapp.ts directly. In v2, channel adapters are installed from the channels branch via /add-<channel> skills — not present on trunk by default. Creates false expectation.
